modules.notes: Exact theorem anchor is working and export/report state is coherent, but this lab is intentionally narrow and does not expose unsupported free-form input.
Known exact anchor reproduces correctly; this lab is trustworthy for the tracked sector example but not yet a broad QEC explorer.

modules.scenario: periodic projection versus short GLM run
modules.notes: Real numerical comparison is present and the exact-versus-GLM gap is preserved.

modules.scenario: periodic exact branch plus bounded transplant failure
modules.notes: This lab is trustworthy for both the periodic exact anchor and the bounded-domain negative benchmark.
The lab behaves correctly on the kept projection-compatible example, but it remains an anchor surface rather than a broad discovery tool.

modules.scenario: invariant-split asymptotic correction with finite-time no-go
modules.notes: The lab correctly distinguishes asymptotic suppression from impossible finite-time exact recovery.
No-Go Explorer is trustworthy for explicit counterexamples and does not invent fixes for structurally rejected setups.
The recoverability studio is trustworthy for theorem-backed and family-backed threshold cases and keeps fix cards tied to real comparisons.

modules.scenario: wrong architecture on bounded-domain protected class
modules.notes: This is currently the strongest end-to-end diagnosis-to-repair workflow in the workbench.

modules.scenario: typed restricted-linear failure, repair, and unsupported nonlinear rejection
modules.notes: The mixer is strong enough for supported typed composition and explicit unsupported handling; it is not a free symbolic sandbox.

modules.notes: Benchmark console exports real demo rows and module-health summaries.

findings.title: Random periodic modal search supports the support-threshold rule
findings.summary: Across 40 seeded random periodic mixer cases, the tool's predicted minimum cutoff matched the highest active modal support index every time on the tracked four-mode basis.
findings.support: family-specific empirical extension of the periodic support-threshold story; independently checked by parsing the generated modal functional support.

findings.title: Naive active-sensor-count heuristic fails in the diagonal/history family
findings.summary: Seed 46 produced a control case where the naive active-sensor-count heuristic predicts horizon 4, but the tool and independent diagonal-family logic require horizon 1.
findings.support: supports keeping the control threshold logic tied to interpolation structure rather than raw sensor-count language.

findings.title: Random linear mixer is usable for repairable counterexample discovery
findings.summary: Seeded restricted-linear search continues to produce real repairable failures with exact after-fixes, making it suitable for guided discovery inside the supported family.
findings.support: tool finding only; this is a validation of discovery usefulness, not a new theorem claim.
